Edward Heath resigned as Prime Minister following a hung parliament and was succeeded by Harold Wilson, Victoria Beckham was born, and ABBA won the Eurovision Song Contest with 'Waterloo'. In what year did these events occur?

Figure skater Nancy Kerrigan was attacked by someone hired by her rival, Tonya Harding's ex-husband, Schindler's List won seven Oscars including Best Picture and Best Director for Steven Spielberg, and the FIFA World Cup was held in the USA with Brazil defeating Italy in the final. In what year did these events occur?

Liverpool won their 18th Football League title with Aston Villa as runners-up, the Community Charge (Poll Tax) was introduced sparking widespread rioting in many parts of the UK, and West Germany won the FIFA World Cup when they defeated Argentina 1-0 in Rome. In what year did these events happen?
The world's tallest bridge, the Millau Viaduct, was officially opened. Actress Millie Bobby Brown and tennis player Coco Gauff were born this year. Arsenal beat Chelsea to the Premier League title.

Janis Joplin died at the age of 27. Edward Heath became Prime Minister after the Conservative party won the election, replacing Harold Wilson's Labour government. Thor Heyerdahl sailed across the Atlantic in the papyrus boat Ra II.

The word processor software that would become Microsoft Word was first released. Protests from peace campaigners started at RAF Greenham Common as cruise missiles arrived. £26 million was stolen from the Brink's-Mat vaults at Heathrow.
Margaret Thatcher resigned and was replaced by John Major. Footballer Joe Allen and actress Emma Watson were born this year. 'Nothing Compares 2 U' by Sinead O'Connor spent four weeks at No. 1.
Richard Nixon resigned in August following the Watergate Scandal. Ceefax was started by the BBC. West Germany beat The Netherlands 2-1 to win the World Cup.

Jamaican singer Bob Marley died at the age of 36. A TV audience of 750 million watched the wedding of Prince Charles to Lady Diana Spencer. Mark David Chapman was sentenced to 20 years for the murder of John Lennon.

Aberdeen FC beat Real Madrid to win the European Cup Winners Cup, Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' was shown on MTV for the first time, and 'Flashdance' starring Jennifer Beals was a box office hit. In what year did these events occur?

The Terracotta Army was discovered in Xi'an, China; Muhammad Ali knocked out George Foreman in Zaire to regain the Heavyweight title during the 'Rumble in the Jungle'; and Erno Rubik invented his Cube. In what year did these events occur?
'Vogue' spent four weeks at No. 1 in the UK charts, Nelson Mandela was released after 27 years, and Iraq invaded Kuwait leading to the Gulf War. In what year did these events occur?
The winter Olympics were held in Turin, Italy; the website Twitter was launched; and Lordi won the Eurovision Song Contest with 'Hard Rock Hallelujah'. In what year did these events occur?

Chariots of Fire won the Best Picture Oscar and three other Oscars; the Argentine warship, The General Belgrano, was sunk by a British submarine; and Canada gained full political independence from the United Kingdom. In what year did these events occur?

Ronald Reagan was sworn in for his second term as President of the USA; the border between Gibraltar and Spain opened for the first time since being closed by Franco in 1969; and the first episode of Eastenders was broadcast on BBC One. In what year did these events occur?
